title: Crispy Almond & Apple Fritters
summary: These tender and crispy fritters are stuffed with apples, flaked almonds and a drizzle of Rude Health Almond Drink.

ingredients.label: 200ml Rude Health Almond Drink, Plus 3 Tbsp
ingredients.name: Almond Milk

method.description: Chop up the apples into cubes, leave the skins on. Pop these into a bowl and grate in the ginger.
Add the juice of half a lemon with the light brown sugar, cinnamon, plain flour, oats, baking powder, 200ml of Rude Health Almond Drink, and ½ tsp salt. Mix well to make a thick batter.
Add vegetable oil to a large pan (roughly enough to deep-fry your fritters in) and bring it to a medium heat.
Using two spoons, spoon large blobs of mixture into the oil and fry for 5-6 mins until dark brown but not burnt. A slower medium fry will make them crispier.
While your fritters are crisping up, make your glaze by whisking icing sugar with 3 tbsp of Rude Health Almond Drink to form a smooth paste.
Drain your fritters, allowing them to cool slightly on paper towels to remove any excess oil, before dunking them in the glaze. If you don’t want them too sweet, drizzle the glaze on instead.
Enjoy hot with a cup of tea.

chefs.name: Seema Pankhania
chefs.summary: Seema Pankhania (aka @seemagetsbaked) is a superstar food creator, with over 1.2M followers and 21.8M Likes on TikTok. After training at Gordon Ramsay's Lucky Cat restaurant, Seema embarked on a presenting career at Mob, where she was responsible for many of their most popular recipes.
